hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance SSTR2-specific ECL2/TM5 complementarity, while mutating position 6 to acidic residues (E/D) will disrupt off-target (SSTR3/SSTR5) electrostatic interactions without sacrificing SSTR2 binding. This combination will increase the selectivity margin (Δmargin) by differentiating SSTR2 from off-targets.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current designs are achieving strong binding but are structurally suboptimal. Tightening structural constraints and introducing targeted mutations at non-pharmacophore positions will improve pass rates and further enhance selectivity.

proposed_changes.rationale: Reduce the allowed clash score to improve structural quality and reduce false positives with poor geometry.
Focus mutations on non-pharmacophore positions to disrupt off-target contacts while preserving SSTR2 pharmacophore (FWKT7-10 and Cys3/Cys14).
